name: Southwestern Stuffed Peppers
recipeIngredient: 6 large Green, Red or Yellow Peppers
1/2 pound Italian sausage or chorizo (remove casing)
1 package (8 ounces) Pepperidge Farm Corn Bread Stuffing Mix
2 cups Shredded Monterey Jack cheese with jalapeno
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare 14-1/2 ounce can of Swanson Chicken Broth.
2. Chop 1 large Red pepper into small pieces.
3. Using a sharp knife, carefully slice the tops off the 6 peppers and discard the seeds and membranes.
4. Place the peppers in a 5-quart saucepan with enough boiling salted water to cover them. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 5 minutes. Drain the peppers on paper towels and empty the water from the saucepan.
5. In a 10-inch skillet over medium heat, brown the sausage and garlic, stirring to separate the meat.
6. In a large bowl, combine the sausage, stuffing mix, cheese, and 3/4 cup of the broth. Mix well.
7. Fill the peppers with the stuffing mixture.
8. In the same saucepan used for boiling the peppers, combine the remaining broth and chopped red pepper. Arrange the stuffed peppers in the broth and heat to boiling. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 minutes or until the peppers are tender and the filling is hot. Remove the stuffed peppers to a serving platter and keep warm.
9. In a covered blender container, carefully blend the chopped red pepper and remaining broth mixture until smooth.
10. Serves 6.
Makes 6 servings.
